title: Claude 5.1 Confirmed: What Happens After The Fable 5 Ban?
summary: A YouTube video claiming Claude 5 (codenamed Fable) was banned by the U.S. government 76 hours after launch due to superior capabilities (80.3% on SWE-Bench Pro, large codebase migration), with speculation about covert capability throttling, Commerce Department involvement, and competitive positioning versus GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.5 Flash, and DeepSeek V4.
writeup: The video claims that Claude 5, codenamed Fable, was banned by the U.S. government 76 hours after launch because of its capabilities, citing an 80.3% score on SWE-Bench Pro and a large codebase migration as evidence. It speculates about covert capability throttling, Commerce Department involvement, and how the model would stack up against GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.5 Flash, and DeepSeek V4. The framing touches on test-time compute, code generation, capability gating, and model regulation. The specifics are presented as claims and speculation rather than confirmed reporting.
